stemHtml: Is there, by convention, a difference between an INS and an IRS?

options.a: Yes, the INS requires electrical power for its operation, while the IRS is completely independent of the electrical power.
options.b: Yes, the IRS is an older system based on a stabilised platform and mechanical gyros, while the INS is a more modern, fixed system based on ring laser gyros or fibre optic gyros.
options.c: Yes, the INS is an older system based on a stabilised platform and mechanical gyros, while the IRS is a more modern, fixed system based on ring laser gyros or fibre optic gyros.
options.d: There is NO actual difference between an INS and an IRS, they are just different abbreviations used by different system manufacturers.
